turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having some chest tightness lately, a pressure sensation in my chest that's been going on for about three weeks now. It's really got me worried because I'm not used to feeling this way.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: It's like someone is squeezing my chest, a tight pressure sensation that's been building up over three weeks. It's substernal, right in the middle of my chest, and it doesn't feel like any discomfort I've ever had before - it's more like a constant weight.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about three weeks ago. I was driving back from a delivery, felt fine during the trip, but when I stopped for dinner, that's when the tightness began. It's been there ever since, getting worse on exertion and making it hard to breathe sometimes.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens pretty much every day now, but not all the time. I'd say 8 out of 10 days, when I'm driving or doing something physical, the tightness comes back, especially after exertion like climbing stairs or lifting heavy things.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's probably an 8 out of 10, I'd say. The worst days are when I'm driving and have to sit for hours, that's when the pressure feels the strongest, like someone is squeezing my chest real tight.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: Yes, when the chest tightness hits, I also start sweating a lot, my heart beats faster, and I feel like I'm getting winded even when I'm just sitting still. The sweating is usually on my upper body, underarms and forehead, it's pretty noticeable.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: Yes, it definitely gets worse with exertion, like when I'm driving for long periods or lifting heavy things. The pressure in my chest feels like it increases and makes it harder to breathe, and that's when the sweating and rapid heartbeat are most noticeable.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: Yes, I do get some sweating during these episodes. It's a lot of sweating, mostly on my upper body like my underarms and forehead. And yes, I also feel a little nauseous, but it's more of a mild feeling, not bad enough to make me want to vomit.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: Yes, during the more severe episodes, I've noticed that my left arm tingles, it feels numb and sort of prickly. It's like pins and needles but on my entire left arm, not just in one spot.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: Actually, yes, I've noticed that my ankles feel a bit swollen lately. My shoes even feel tighter than usual because of it.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I'm taking Amlodipine 10mg daily for my high blood pressure, but I have to admit that I don't always take it as prescribed. My schedule on the road can be pretty irregular, so I sometimes miss doses or forget to take them at the right time. Other than that, I also take Metformin 1000mg twice a day for my diabetes, and I'm fairly consistent with those doses.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: Yes, I've been diagnosed with hypertension for about 8 years now, and type 2 diabetes for about 5 years. I take Metformin twice daily for my diabetes, and I'm supposed to take Amlodipine once a day for my blood pressure, but like I said, it's not always consistent because of my irregular schedule on the road.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: Yes, I was diagnosed with hyperlipidemia about 3 years ago and was prescribed atorvastatin. However, I stopped taking it a year ago because... well, to be honest, I just got tired of taking another medication and wasn't feeling any symptoms at the time, so I thought I could stop.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I do smoke, actually. It's been a habit for 25 years now, but I've tried to cut back over the years. I'd say I smoke about half a pack of cigarettes per day. And as for drinking, I'll have maybe one or two beers on the weekends when I'm home, nothing too excessive.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: To be honest, my diet isn't great. I eat a lot of fast food, probably 5-6 days out of the week. It's just convenient when I'm on the road, you know? And as for exercise... well, to be blunt, I don't really do any. My job is pretty sedentary, and even when I'm not working, I tend to just sit around at home or while driving.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: Yes... my father passed away from a heart attack at 52. It was a few years ago, but it's still on my mind because of his age and how sudden it happened. It makes me worry about my own health even more now that I'm experiencing these chest tightness episodes.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My mother actually has hypertension and type 2 diabetes, but she's doing okay at her age. She's alive and kicking at 78.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: About a year ago, I went in for my routine checkup and they told me my cholesterol was high, but that was it. Nothing else significant came out of that visit.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: No known drug allergies.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: I've been noticing that my shoes feel tighter than usual lately, and my ankles are a bit swollen. Other than that, I'm not sure if it's relevant, but I do have a history of high cholesterol, which was diagnosed during my last checkup about a year ago. They told me to work on my diet and exercise habits to get it under control, but like I said, I haven't really made any changes yet.
